data.body:
## Context
Developers working in worktrees experience unnecessary friction when merging from main — the pre-commit test gate requires re-running tests for ALL files brought in by the merge, even though those files were already tested on main's CI. During a recent 92-file merge, this required multiple rounds of record-test-status.sh + re-commit attempts. The review gate already handles this correctly by filtering out incoming-only files, but the test gate lacks the same awareness.

## Success Criteria
1. When committing a merge (MERGE_HEAD exists), the test gate only enforces tests for files that the worktree branch modified — not files that came in cleanly from main
2. When the filtered file set is empty (merge brought only incoming-only files), the test gate exits 0 without requiring record-test-status.sh
3. When merge-base computation fails (e.g., corrupt MERGE_HEAD), the test gate falls through to normal full enforcement (fail-safe)
4. Existing non-merge commits are unaffected — the test gate enforces identically to today
5. Before/after validation: a merge commit that brings in 10+ files from main where the worktree branch touched none of them completes the test gate in a single git commit without requiring record-test-status.sh

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Mirror the review gate's merge-aware filtering pattern (pre-commit-review-gate.sh lines 180-206) into pre-commit-test-gate.sh. Compute merge base between HEAD and MERGE_HEAD, identify files changed on the worktree branch (merge-base..HEAD), and filter staged files to only those the worktree branch touched. Same fail-safe: if merge-base computation fails, fall through to normal enforcement.
